writer@laptop — Social media writer and community engagement agent. Drafts tweets, posts to Hacker News and similar communities, tracks outreach history, and builds ongoing engagement.  [spawnable via claude --dangerously-skip-permissions] [...] **The fix:** `who --all-projects` fell back to the old `WhoSnapshot`/`render_who_*` markdown-table renderer because the daemon's `rpc_who` only builds the unified fabric view when a single project scope resolves — `--all-projects` has no single scope, so that branch was skipped entirely (this was a known, documented open tail from the 2026-06-29 session that unified `who` with hook injection). I added `render_fabric_all_projects`/`_human` in `fabric_context.rs` (one project block per root channel, roster rendered once up front) and wired it into the `all_projects` branch of `rpc_who`. Compiled clean, clippy clean, all existing `who`/`fabric_context` unit tests pass, and a new isolated integration test confirms the unified format renders (still finishing a wrinkle in that test around session-id anchoring for the second project).
